sub: A fictional Soul-Steel consumer that adds character + workflow kinds — the test bed proving the framework renders a project it has never seen, with zero engine changes.
axis: 1 CONTRACT → 2 KINDS → VALIDATOR · SCAFFOLDER · GUARD → DERIVED DASHBOARD
sourceLine: source-of-truth: schema/project-entity.base.schema.json + soul-steel-demo's validated _project/ entities · generated by tools/render-hub.mjs
strategy: One contract, three primitives, many disposable renderers — the planning layer reduced the way a UI reduces to State and Relation.

domains.name: ProjectEntity
domains.note: One non-optional contract every _project/ file derives from — Identity (kind·id·title) · State (status·updated) · Relation (links). kind & id are loader-derived from the path; the authored surface is just title/status/updated (+links/tags). The validator, scaffolder, guard, and this dashboard all read this single source.

domains.name: Cast
domains.note: 1 character entity — status over the per-kind enum (all concept). 0 typed links touch this kind.

domains.name: Swimlanes
domains.note: 2 workflow entities — status over the per-kind enum (all draft). 1 typed link touch this kind.

domains.name: Triad
domains.note: Three zero-dependency engines, all reading the one contract via lib/contract.mjs (no check re-implemented): the validator (per-kind enums, link resolution, prose↔frontmatter), the scaffolder behind /new <kind>, and the PreToolUse guard that blocks any _project/ write that would break the contract.
